---
title: "Notebook: Probability and Moments"
lang: en
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: lualatex
jupyter: python3
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This notebook continues the opening chapter with a small toy dataset.

## Toy sample

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

x = np.array([1.2, 0.7, 1.5, 2.1, 0.9, 1.8, 1.1, 1.4])
summary = pd.DataFrame({
    "observation": np.arange(1, len(x) + 1),
    "value": x,
})
summary
```

## Sample moments

```{python}
n = len(x)
xbar = x.mean()
m2 = np.mean((x - xbar) ** 2)
m3 = np.mean((x - xbar) ** 3)
m4 = np.mean((x - xbar) ** 4)

pd.DataFrame({
    "quantity": ["n", "xbar", "m2", "m3", "m4"],
    "value": [n, xbar, m2, m3, m4],
})
```

## Quick plot

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6.2, 4.0))
ax.hist(x, bins=5, color="#2f6c8f", edgecolor="white")
ax.axvline(xbar, color="#c44536", linestyle="--", linewidth=2)
ax.set_xlabel("Value")
ax.set_ylabel("Count")
ax.set_title("Toy sample")
plt.show()
```
